title: Tender Algerian Fish Tagine
description: A mustardy and gooey Algerian dish featuring cinnamon, perfect for a post-workout meal, made with fish and simmered to perfection in a mason jar. This pescatarian-friendly recipe is a great way to refuel after a workout.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large mason jar, heat the olive oil over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion to the mason jar and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic to the mason jar and cook for an additional 1 minute.
Add the cod fish pieces to the mason jar and cook until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes.
In a small bowl, mix together the ground cinnamon, ground cumin, smoked paprika, salt, and black pepper.
Add the spice mixture to the mason jar and stir to combine.
Add the whole wheat couscous to the mason jar and stir to combine.
Add the dijon mustard to the mason jar and stir to combine.
Cover the mason jar with a lid and transfer to the preheated oven.
Bake for 20 minutes, or until the fish is cooked through and the couscous is tender.
